rationale: The page provides detailed information on Antimicrobial Resistance (AMR) drivers and Fixed-Dose Combination (FDC) drugs, including their definition, benefits, and the regulatory framework for pharmaceutical products in India. This is high-quality content for generating questions on public health and pharmaceutical regulation.
cleanedText: AMR is a natural process that happens over time through genetic changes in pathogens. However, its emergence and spread are accelerated due to several anthropogenic factors. Strains of bacteria, viruses, parasites, and fungi that are resistant to most antimicrobials are called Superbugs. Spread through vertical gene transfer during bacterial division and through horizontal gene transfer by exchange of resistance genes among microbes. WHO recognized AMR as one of the top ten global health threats. It is often described as a silent pandemic. Key Drivers of AMR: Misuse and overuse of antibiotics in humans (Patients frequently self-prescribe antibiotics owing to their over-the-counter availability); Agriculture & Allied Sector (Use of antimicrobials in intensive farming and the Livestock sector used as growth-promoters); Pharmaceutical Manufacturing (Industrial waste from the production of Active Pharmaceutical Ingredients (APIs) for antibiotics); Healthcare Facilities (Improper management of unused medications, patient excretion, and disposal of expired drugs); Poor Waste Management (Landfill leachate, untreated wastewater, etc.); Other (Prematurely discontinue treatment courses, etc.). 5.4. FIXED-DOSE COMBINATION (FDC) DRUGS. Why in the News? Central Drugs Standard Control Organisation (CDSCO) has directed all states and Union Territories to stop the manufacture, sale, and distribution of 35 unapproved FDC drugs. About Fixed-Dose Combination (FDC) Drugs: FDC refer to products containing one or more active ingredients used for a particular indication(s). Active Ingredient is the biologically active component of a drug product (tablet, capsule, cream, injectable) that produces the intended effects. FDCs are considered new drug (under NDCT Rules, 2019) when it meets two conditions: It combines two or more drugs already approved for individual use in a fixed ratio; It alters the ratio of drugs in an already approved FDC, along with making changes to its claims. Why FDC drugs are Preferred? Better Therapeutic Outcomes (Enhanced synergistic effects between combined drugs when rationally selected); Improved Patient Compliance (Reduces the pill burden); Reduced Cost of Therapy (Combined products may lower costs compared to purchasing multiple individual drugs). Regulation of Pharma Products in India: Drugs and Cosmetics Act, 1940 & Drugs and Cosmetics Rules, 1945 governs import, manufacture, distribution, and sale of drugs and cosmetics in India. Central Drugs Standard Control Organisation (CDSCO) under Directorate General of Health Services, Ministry of Health & Family Welfare is National Regulatory Authority of India. Under Drugs and Cosmetics Act, CDSCO is responsible for approval of Drugs, Conduct of Clinical Trials, laying down the standards for Drugs, control over the quality of imported Drugs in country. State Drug Regulatory Authorities are empowered to grant licenses, conduct inspections, and ensure compliance with drug laws at state level. National Pharmaceutical Pricing Authority (NPPA) enforces provisions of Drugs (Prices Control) Order. Good Manufacturing Practices (GMP): Schedule M of Drugs and Cosmetics Rules prescribes Good Manufacturing Practices (GMP), which align with WHO guidelines.
